title: Umami Smoked Italian Pork Polenta
description: A savory, shaggy, adventurous Italian (Piedmontese) dish featuring sweet sherry and a smoke-cold flavor profile, halal-friendly.
instructions: Preheat the smoker to 225°F (110°C) using your preferred type of wood for smoking.
Season the pork shoulder with salt, black pepper, and smoked paprika.
Sear the pork shoulder in olive oil over medium-high heat until browned on all sides, then transfer it to the smoker.
Smoke the pork shoulder for 6 hours or until it reaches an internal temperature of 160°F (71°C).
While the pork is smoking, combine the polenta cornmeal, porcini mushrooms, and 4 cups of water in a large pot.
Bring the mixture to a boil over high heat, then reduce the heat to medium and simmer for 20 minutes.
Stir in sweet sherry and cook for an additional 2 minutes.
Shred the smoked pork shoulder with two forks and add it to the polenta mixture.
Stir in chopped onion and minced garlic.
Season the polenta with salt to taste.
Serve the Umami Smoked Italian Pork Polenta hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs if desired.
